checks.finding: ## Architecture (design.md L20-38) provides all three required elements: (1) top-level component boundaries via mermaid flowchart (MCP client -> server.ts tool registration -> tools/evidence.ts evidenceDeepVerify -> parseEvidenceBundle/parseVerificationContract -> path-guard guardedRead -> envelope); (2) integration with existing components stated ('既存 5 ツールと同型', reuse of parseEvidenceBundle/parseVerificationContract/path-guard/envelope, key denylist node); (3) per-layer technology in the Components table (L40-51: TypeScript, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, node:crypto SHA-256, JSON Schema). Interactions (per-artifact recompute, invariant recompute, denylist) are described, not merely named.

Explicit dependency declarations exist in the Architecture mermaid (L22-34) and Components table (L40-51). The directed graph server.ts -> evidence.ts -> {parseEvidenceBundle, parseVerificationContract, path-guard, envelope}, with path-guard/envelope as leaves, is acyclic; no component depends back on a predecessor. No cycle detected.

## Data Plan (design.md L78-89) specifies all three sub-fields: Data Entities ('なし / 永続データを持たない', with read targets enumerated: <feature>/verification/<taskId>.evidence.json, artifacts[].path, quality_report, verification_contract, spec files); Existing Data Affected ('なし / 書込みなし'); Migration Strategy ('不要 / read-only ツール追加', additive v1 contract, backward-compatible). Explicit 'no data changes' rationale present per sub-field.

## API / Contract Plan (design.md L91-194) fully specifies the single new interface: tool evidence_deep_verify with input schema {feature: string, taskId: string} (zod, L95), complete response schema evidenceDeepVerifyData (L99-118: verdict, artifacts[] statuses, invariants, signature, failures), error envelope mapping (invalid-input/not-found/cannot-parse, L123-124), and explicit backward-compat/non-breaking statement (additive to okEnvelope.data.oneOf, v1 maintained, L88-89, L235). Deprecation/breaking-change status is addressed ('破壊的変更でない').

Security boundaries are expressed. design.md delegates to the normative security-spec.md via its Layer Specifications table (L60, Security -> security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, owner+status Planned) and carries inline controls: Architecture (L36-38 no-exec/no-write/no-net, key denylist node L32), the '署名の扱い(REQ-008 の中心統制)' subsection (L188-194), and Constraint Compliance security row (L230). Treating security-spec.md as a normative refinement, all four required items are present: every trust boundary named (B1 client->tool, B2 tool->filesystem, B3 tool->key material; security-spec.md Trust Boundaries table L20-24); auth/authz per boundary (OS user boundary, path-guard allowlist/denylist, key read-forbidden; L22-24, Authorization table L45-50); PII/data classification (internal for bundle data, restricted for key material 'not handled'; Data Classification table L54-58); and OWASP Top 10 coverage (Cryptographic Failures, Broken Access Control, Injection, Software & Data Integrity Failures, Logging; OWASP Mapping L62-68). No coverage gap.

SKIP: Feature Type is api-only (design.md L4). This is a read-only MCP tool server with no frontend surface (ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md are N/A/minimal-by-design; Layer Specifications L57-58 mark UX/Frontend 'N/A — no change'). No frontend plan to reconcile against the API contract.

## Test Strategy (design.md L196-213) covers all test levels with concrete planned evidence: unit (determinism byte-equal, contract ajv), integration (tamper: 1-byte/recorded-sha/spec-drift; error-path: missing/too-large/invalid-recorded-sha), acceptance/host-parity (golden parity mapping to AC-012), plus named tooling and artifacts (node:test, ajv against contracts/sdd-forge-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json, static readonly checks in tests/readonly, tests/golden, MCP Inspector CLI tools/list smoke, dist-parity CI in .github/workflows/test.yml). acceptance-tests.md maps every AC-001..AC-019 to a TEST-NNN and concrete test target directory. For this api-only feature the equivalent of a journey path is the host-script parity golden (AC-012) and the tools/list smoke (AC-016).

Every component referenced in Architecture and API/Contract Plan is defined in the ## Components table (design.md L40-51): tools/evidence.ts (evidenceDeepVerify), server.ts, parsers/evidence.ts (parseEvidenceBundle/parseVerificationContract), parsers/evidence-bundle.ts (EvidenceBundle/EvidenceArtifact types), path-guard.ts (guardedRead/resolveGuarded), envelope.ts (ok/err/Result), and contracts/sdd-forge-mcp-tools.v1.schema.json. The ~/.sdd/evidence-key node is a denylist target (asset), not an implementation component requiring definition. All referenced components are either defined here or are established existing sdd-forge-mcp modules identifiable from requirements.md context (Issue #60 baseline).

## ADR Change Log (design.md L71-76) references ADR-0008 and ADR-0009; both exist as files: docs/adr/0008-evidence-deep-verify-no-signature-crypto.md and docs/adr/0009-evidence-deep-verify-match-host-canonical-formulas.md (verified present). The two new architectural decisions this feature introduces — no-signature-crypto / host-deferred git ancestry (ADR-0008) and verbatim match of host canonical formulas (ADR-0009) — are each covered by a corresponding new ADR. No uncovered architectural departure.
